title: Interconnected Reality
definition: The notion that all aspects of reality are deeply interconnected and influence each other in intricate ways.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Just like the dots on a connect-the-dots picture are all connected, everything in the universe is connected to each other in some way.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The idea that reality emerges from complex interactions between its parts and that these interactions create new properties at different scales, such as thermodynamics, life, and consciousness.
difficulty_levels.expert: The philosophical concept that reality is not a collection of independent entities but rather an interconnected network where changes in one part can have far-reaching effects on others. This concept is supported by observations in various fields, such as science, sociology, and data science.

extra_fields.insight: The understanding that our current fragmented knowledge limits our perception of the deeply interconnected reality we live in, and that a collective self-awareness could help us overcome this limitation.
